xEnrich 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Location-aware enrichment pipeline

Source: R/run_location_pipeline.R
run_location_pipeline.Rd

Implements a two-step enrichment strategy that moves from organ-level signal to cell-type resolution. Step 1 uses Human Protein Atlas (HPA) data (IHC or RNA) to identify enriched organs. Step 2 drills into the top organs using Tabula Sapiens scRNA data to identify which cell types drive the signal.

run_location_pipeline(
  gene_list,
  hpa_data,
  tabula_data,
  hpa_type = c("ihc", "rna"),
  top_n_organs = 3L,
  max_organ_padj = 0.05,
  hpa_args = list(),
  tabula_args = list(),
  seed = NULL
)

Arguments

gene_list

Character vector of gene symbols (the query set).

hpa_data

Data frame of HPA data passed to the HPA enrichment step.

tabula_data

Named list of Tabula Sapiens data frames, one per organ. Names must match the tabula column of the bridge table. See load_reference() to load datasets.

hpa_type

Character. "ihc" (default, uses enrich_by_hpa_ihc()) or "rna" (uses enrich_by_hpa()).

top_n_organs

Integer. Maximum number of significant organs to drill into. Default 3.

max_organ_padj

Numeric. Adjusted p-value ceiling for organ selection. Only organs with padj <= max_organ_padj proceed to the Tabula step. Default 0.05.

hpa_args

Named list of arguments passed to enrich_by_hpa_ihc() or enrich_by_hpa(). Do not include gene_list or reference_df these are supplied internally.

tabula_args

Named list of arguments passed to enrich_by_tabula(). Do not include gene_list or reference_df.

seed

Optional integer. Applied once before the pipeline starts, ensuring reproducibility across both steps.

Value

An object of class xEnrich_pipeline, a list containing:

organ_results

Data frame of HPA enrichment results across all groups (full output of the HPA step).

celltype_results

Named list of data frames, one per organ that was successfully mapped and tested. Each data frame is the output of enrich_by_tabula() for that organ.

selected_organs

Character vector of organs that passed max_organ_padj and were mapped to Tabula.

bridge_used

Data frame of the organ→Tabula mappings actually used, including the approximate flag.

hpa_type

Character. HPA step type used.

call_args

List recording top_n_organs, max_organ_padj, hpa_args, tabula_args for reproducibility.

Details

This pipeline operationalises the central dogma-aware enrichment concept: IHC data provides protein-level organ evidence; scRNA data resolves cellular origin. Together they answer not just "what pathway" but "where in the body and in which cell type."

Pipeline steps

  1. Run enrich_by_hpa_ihc() (if hpa_type = "ihc") or enrich_by_hpa() (if hpa_type = "rna") on hpa_data.

  2. Select up to top_n_organs organs with padj <= max_organ_padj, ordered by adjusted p-value.

  3. Map organ names to Tabula dataset names via the internal tabula_hpa_bridge lookup. Approximate mappings (e.g. trachea → "Bronchus") are flagged in the output.

  4. For each matched organ, run enrich_by_tabula() on the corresponding dataset (loaded from tabula_data).

  5. Return a structured list with organ-level and cell-type-level results.

Providing Tabula data

Pass pre-loaded datasets as a named list via tabula_data: list(kidney = load_reference("kidney", source = "tabula")[[1]], ...). Dataset names must match the tabula column of the bridge table (e.g. "kidney", "liver"). If a required organ is absent from tabula_data, it is skipped with a message.

Argument passing

Arguments for the HPA step and the Tabula step are passed as named lists via hpa_args and tabula_args respectively. This avoids ambiguity when both steps share parameter names (e.g. method, universe_type). Example:


run_location_pipeline(
  gene_list   = my_genes,
  hpa_data    = ihc_data,
  tabula_data = tabula_list,
  hpa_args    = list(method = "hypergeometric", n_perm = 1000),
  tabula_args = list(method = "gsea", min_pct_expr = 0.15)
)

See also

enrich_by_hpa_ihc(), enrich_by_hpa(), enrich_by_tabula(), load_reference(), print.xEnrich_pipeline(), summary.xEnrich_pipeline()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Load data
hpa_ihc   <- load_reference("ihc",    source = "hpa")[[1]]
tabula_kd <- load_reference("kidney", source = "tabula")[[1]]
tabula_lv <- load_reference("liver",  source = "tabula")[[1]]

gene_list <- c("UMOD", "SLC12A1", "NPHS1", "PODXL", "CDH16")

result <- run_location_pipeline(
  gene_list   = gene_list,
  hpa_data    = hpa_ihc,
  tabula_data = list(kidney = tabula_kd, liver = tabula_lv),
  hpa_type    = "ihc",
  top_n_organs = 3,
  hpa_args    = list(method = "hypergeometric"),
  tabula_args = list(method = "hypergeometric", min_pct_expr = 0.1),
  seed        = 42
)

print(result)
result$organ_results
result$celltype_results$kidney
} # }
